fingerprints.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan (adds ~$39.95 fee) OR FD-258 hard card via mail (tip: moisturize hands for days prior to reduce smudge rejects)

seo.title: New Jersey Physical Therapist License Reciprocity - How to Transfer Your License (2026)
seo.description: Already licensed as a physical therapist? Here's whether your license transfers to New Jersey - compact status, endorsement steps, fees, and processing time.
seo.keywords: physical therapist license reciprocity new jersey, how to transfer physical therapist license to new jersey, physical therapist compact new jersey, new jersey physical therapist endorsement application

board.name: New Jersey Board of Physical Therapy Examiners
board.parent_dept: New Jersey Division of Consumer Affairs
board.mailing_address: Board of Physical Therapy Examiners, PO Box 45014, Newark, New Jersey 07101

license_types.title: PT, DPT
license_types.description: The standard credential to practice as a physical therapist in New Jersey.

steps.title: Create an Account
steps.description: Navigate to the **NJ Consumer Affairs Online Licensing System** (newjersey.mylicense.com/boards/NJ/PT/OnlineServices/AppLogin.aspx) and register a new user account if you are a first-time applicant.

steps.title: Submit Online Application
steps.description: Log in to the **NJ Consumer Affairs Online Licensing System**, select 'Apply for a New License,' and complete the comprehensive Physical Therapist application form, providing all required personal, educational, and professional details.

steps.title: Arrange for Supporting Documents
steps.description: Ensure official transcripts from your physical therapy program are sent directly to the Board, submit your NPTE scores, complete the mandatory Criminal History Background Check (CHBC) as instructed after application, and successfully pass the online Jurisprudence Examination.

steps.title: Pay Required Fees
steps.description: Remit the non-refundable application fee ($125) and the initial license fee (either $120 or $60, depending on the biennial cycle) through the **NJ Consumer Affairs Online Licensing System**.

steps.title: Monitor Application Status
steps.description: Regularly check your application's progress and respond to any requests for additional information from the Board of Physical Therapy Examiners via your account on the **NJ Consumer Affairs Online Licensing System**.

documents.detail: Sent directly from your educational institution.
Official verification of passing the NPTE (National Physical Therapy Examination).

provisional_text: Many states offer a limited permit or provisional license for new graduates waiting to take the NPTE. Check specific board rules for supervisory requirements.
reciprocity_text: Physical Therapists licensed in another state can often apply via endorsement by providing license verification and current certification.

faqs.question: How much does a New Jersey physical therapist license cost?
faqs.answer: For endorsement applicants, the state licensing fee is $125. Additional third-party costs (fingerprinting, transcripts, or verification) may apply.

faqs.question: Is national certification required?
faqs.answer: Yes, passing the NPTE (National Physical Therapy Examination) is a standard requirement for initial licensure across almost all states.

faqs.question: Does New Jersey have reciprocity for physical therapists?
faqs.answer: Yes. New Jersey participates in the PT Compact. If your home state and license qualify for compact privilege, you can typically practice in New Jersey without a separate endorsement application.

faqs.question: How long does it take to transfer my physical therapist license to New Jersey?
faqs.answer: Current board processing time is 4 to 6 Weeks once your file is complete.

faqs.question: Can I start working in New Jersey while my endorsement application is processing?
faqs.answer: A temporary or provisional credential is available while the full endorsement application is pending.

reciprocity.notes: Physical Therapists licensed in another state can often apply via endorsement by providing license verification and current certification.

hero.title: Become a Physical Therapist in New Jersey
hero.dek: To apply for a Physical Therapist license in New Jersey, you must use the NJ Consumer Affairs Online Licensing System to submit your application, supporting documents, and fees.
